skeletons.template:
class Dog:
    # Class attribute
    species = "Canis familiaris"

    def __init__(self, name: str, age: int):
        # Instance attributes
        self.name = name
        self.age = age

    def __str__(self) -> str:
        return f"{self.name} is {self.age} years old."

    # Instance method
    def bark(self):
        return "Woof!"

# Instantiate the class
my_dog = Dog("Buddy", 5)

# Access attributes and methods
print(my_dog)
print(f"{my_dog.name}'s species is {my_dog.species}.")
print(f"{my_dog.name} says: {my_dog.bark()}")

# List comprehension to get squares of numbers
numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
squares = [n**2 for n in numbers]
print(f"Squares: {squares}")

# List comprehension with a condition
even_numbers = [n for n in numbers if n % 2 == 0]
print(f"Even Numbers: {even_numbers}")

# Dictionary comprehension
words = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
word_lengths = {word: len(word) for word in words}
print(f"Word Lengths: {word_lengths}")

# Set comprehension for unique squares of numbers
nums = [-2, -1, 0, 1, 2]
unique_squares = {n**2 for n in nums}
print(f"Unique Squares: {unique_squares}")

# Make sure to install the library first: pip install requests
import requests

# The URL for the JSONPlaceholder API
url = "https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1"

try:
    # Make the GET request
    response = requests.get(url)

    # Check if the request was successful (status code 200)
    response.raise_for_status()

    # Parse the JSON response
    data = response.json()

    # Print the results
    print(f"Successfully fetched data for post ID: {data['id']}")
    print(f"Title: {data['title']}")
    print(f"\nBody:\n{data['body']}")

except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
    print(f"An error occurred: {e}")

import argparse

# Example Usage:
# python your_script_name.py John -a 30 --verbose
# python your_script_name.py Jane -a 25 -o output.txt

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="A simple command-line argument parser example.")

    # Positional argument
    parser.add_argument("name", type=str, help="The name of the user")

    # Optional argument with a short and long form
    parser.add_argument("-a", "--age", type=int, required=True, help="The age of the user")

    # Optional argument with a default value
    parser.add_argument("-o", "--output", type=str, default="stdout", help="Output file name (default: stdout)")

    # Flag (boolean) argument
    parser.add_argument("-v", "--verbose", action="store_true", help="Enable verbose output")

    args = parser.parse_args()

    if args.verbose:
        print("Verbose mode enabled.")
        print("Parsing command-line arguments...")

    greeting = f"Hello, {args.name}! You are {args.age} years old."

    if args.output == "stdout":
        print(greeting)
    else:
        with open(args.output, "w") as f:
            f.write(greeting + "\n")
        print(f"Output written to {args.output}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
